rulings.Case Title: MARY ANN MEADOWS BALL et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action (Forum Non Conveniens)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants IMO INDUSTRIES INC., and EATON CORPORATION, AS Successor-In-Interest TO EATON ELECTRICAL INC.'s motion to dismiss is denied. Defendants have not established the amenability of all defendants to jurisdiction in Alabama. Defendants¿ motion to stay conditioned on the amenability of all defendants to jurisdiction in Alabama is granted provided that all defendants agree not to assert a wrongful death statute of limitations defense in Alabama. If all defendants do not so agree, the issue turns on whether under Alabama law the pendency of this action tolls the wrongful death statute of limitations in a later-filed Alabama lawsuit. The parties have not briefed this issue and the court¿s research thus far has yet to disclose an answer. In the event that all defendants do not agree not to assert a wrongful death statute of limitations defense in Alabama, then the hearing may be continued for further briefing on the tolling issue. =(220/HEK)

If all defendants do not so agree, the issue turns on whether under Alabama law the pendency of this action tolls the wrongful death statute of limitations in a later-filed Alabama lawsuit. The parties have not briefed this issue and the court¿s research thus far has yet to disclose an answer. In the event that all defendants do not agree not to assert a wrongful death statute of limitations defense in Alabama, then the hearing may be continued for further briefing on the tolling issue. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Dana Corporation¿s motion to dismiss is granted. No opposition filed. A hearing is required unless defense counsel is told by the plaintiff that he does not contest the tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)
